entries.label: Corpus structural diversity
entries.summary: Per-graph structural features (hub_concentration, clustering_coeff, avg_degree, working_set_ratio) for every corpus graph; underpins family classification used by every other report.

entries.label: Per-paper reproduction summary
entries.summary: Per-paper grouped reproduction map (Faldu HPCA20, Balaji HPCA21, Jaleel ISCA10) classifying every claim into ok/within_tolerance/disagree/known_deviation/missing.

entries.label: Literature faithfulness comparator
entries.summary: Cell-level comparator: 288/320 ok (90.0 %), 0 disagree, 30 known_deviation. The single load-bearing aggregate behind every other paper-grade finding.

entries.label: Regression budget floor
entries.summary: Per-cell distance-to-disagree in pp; the smallest margin defines the corpus-wide regression budget. Fails if any cell's budget collapses below the floor.

entries.label: gem5 literature anchor
entries.summary: GRASP-paper L-shape invariants codified per (graph, app) at 4 kB / 256 kB / 2 MB. 16 invariants today, all ok.

entries.label: Sniper literature anchor
entries.summary: Sniper L-shape mirror for PR + SSSP on email-Eu-core + cit-Patents (16 invariants ok; max small-cache spread 6.36 pp). Uses the shared gem5_anchor_summary.py generator.

entries.label: Cross-tool saturation soundness
entries.summary: Pairs each lit-faith cell with its gem5/Sniper anchor at each tool's largest L3 and verifies Δ(GRASP−LRU) sign agreement when doubly saturated.

entries.label: Cross-tool winner agreement
entries.summary: At each tool's largest L3 per (graph, app), do simulators pick the same winning policy? Surfaces 6 split cells (expected; tools sweep disjoint L3 ranges).

entries.label: Policy winner table
entries.summary: Per-cell winner projection: GRASP 56 wins, POPT 41, LRU 6, SRRIP 6 (n=109). Top-5 fragile cells flagged when margin ≤ 1 pp.

entries.label: POPT-vs-GRASP delta
entries.summary: Per-cell Δ(POPT − GRASP) in pp by family/regime. Road family mean −9.276 pp (POPT crushes GRASP); social family mean +0.360 pp (tie).

entries.label: Per-policy oracle gap
entries.summary: Each policy's gap to per-cell empirical oracle (min across 4 policies). Mean gaps: POPT 1.78 pp, GRASP 3.37 pp, SRRIP 3.46 pp, LRU 4.76 pp.

entries.label: Per-kernel oracle gap
entries.summary: Per-(policy, app) oracle-gap matrix exposing per-kernel winners: POPT crushes pr (0.100 pp); GRASP wins cc (0.640 pp); GRASP catastrophic on sssp (7.106 pp). No one-size-fits-all.

entries.label: Per-kernel oracle-gap bootstrap CIs
entries.summary: CI-backed sign claims per kernel: pr→POPT<all P=1.0; cc→GRASP<POPT P=0.9995; bfs→POPT<GRASP P=0.999; sssp→POPT<GRASP P=0.971; bc has no stable ordering among GRASP/POPT/SRRIP.

entries.label: POPT-vs-GRASP per (family x app) CIs
entries.summary: 21 (family,app) cells bootstrapped. Road is POPT-favored on every kernel (sssp -21.8pp, bfs -11.4pp). cc-counter-narrative is CI-strict on social/cc and citation/cc (P≈0.000). social/pr is CI-strict POPT (P=0.9995).

entries.label: Wilson CIs on policy win-counts
entries.summary: Wilson 95% CIs on per-(scope,policy) win-rates. CI-strict majority: pr/POPT [0.529,0.848], cc/GRASP [0.640,0.948]. CI-strict below-chance: cc/POPT [0.000,0.161]. sssp POPT/GRASP not CI-distinguishable.

entries.label: Cohen's h on policy win-rate gaps
entries.summary: 14 large-effect (h≥0.8) dominance pairs. cc/GRASP-vs-POPT h=2.346 (largest). pr/POPT-vs-{LRU,SRRIP} h=2.014. sssp has NO large effect (max h=0.726); pinned as the kernel with weakest policy-ordering signal.

entries.label: Cliff's delta + Mann-Whitney on gap distributions
entries.summary: 10 large-effect (|d|≥0.474) dominance pairs on raw gap_pp distributions. pr/POPT vs LRU d=-0.911 p=0. cc/GRASP dominates all 3 at |d|≥0.474 with MW p<1e-4. Confirms gates 36–37 nonparametrically.

entries.label: Per-L3-size policy stability
entries.summary: Stable single winners at 1MB/4MB/8MB: cc=GRASP, pr=POPT. Regime change: bfs (GRASP@1MB → POPT@≥4MB). No stable cross-L3 winner: sssp. Pinned so paper can never silently average across L3 and hide a regime change.

entries.label: Multiple-testing correction (Holm-Bonferroni + Benjamini-Hochberg)
entries.summary: 81 p-values across gates 34/38 + per-(family,app) combined; HB (FWER) retains 28, BH (FDR) retains 40 at α=0.05. Pins which findings can honestly be called 'significant' after correcting for the family of tests we actually run.

entries.label: Leave-one-graph-out (LOGO) winner robustness
entries.summary: LOGO-robust apps (winner survives every single-graph drop): pr/POPT, cc/GRASP, bc/GRASP. LOGO-fragile: bfs (flips when soc-LiveJournal1 dropped), sssp (flips under com-orkut, roadNet-CA, web-Google drops). Triangulates the cross-gate weak-signal finding for sssp.

entries.label: Cell-winner census (corpus decisiveness)
entries.summary: 114 cells: 97.4% have unique winner, 2.6% tied (3 cells all in bc/email-Eu-core: one 4-way tie + two 2-way ties), 0% no-winner. Pins corpus decisiveness; paper must report the tied subcorpus separately.

entries.label: WSS-relative L3 axis
entries.summary: Bins each cell by L3/WSS ratio (under/near/over WSS). POPT has smallest mean gap in EVERY WSS regime (1.62/2.35/0.22 pp); GRASP wins by count in every regime. Defends against absolute-byte cross-graph comparison pushback.

entries.label: Family-classification sensitivity
entries.summary: Enumerates every (graph, alternative family) relabeling (32 total) and reruns the 7 sign-stability claims. Bedrock: POPT < LRU on social survives 32/32 relabelings. Road claim only loses stability when roadNet-CA (sole road member) is relocated, as expected.

entries.label: Reproducibility smoke
entries.summary: SHA-256-snapshots every tracked wiki/data aggregator, re-runs lit-claims + lit-catalog, asserts byte-identity (modulo declared volatile fields like dashboard runtime_s). Catches silent staleness across the 40-file artifact surface.

entries.label: Winning-regime taxonomy
entries.summary: (graph_family × L3 regime) winner matrix with auto-extracted ≥80 % dominance rules. Headline rules: mesh/all → POPT 100 %; road/large → LRU 75 %.

entries.label: Small-L3 thrash report
entries.summary: 4 kB-L3 sweep (9 (graph, app) cells × 9 policy variants). LRU wins 5/9 cells; GRASP regresses up to +35.857 pp vs LRU on soc-LiveJournal1/bfs.

entries.label: Literature deviations inventory
entries.summary: Closed-vocab mechanism classifier for known_deviation rows: 30/30 classify as popt_overhead_dominates — the exact inverse of road-graph finding.

entries.label: Per-graph claim density
entries.summary: Per-graph literature claim density (8 graphs, 320 claims, 288 OK = 90.0 %). Density per graph: 2 (delaunay_n19) → 12 (cit-Patents).

entries.label: Bootstrap CIs on load-bearing claims
entries.summary: Percentile bootstrap (5000 resamples, seed 1729) on every (policy, family) and (policy, regime) oracle-gap bucket + paired ΔPOPT−GRASP per family + sign-stability fractions. Road POPT < GRASP survives 97.6 % of resamples; social/citation/web do not.

entries.label: Paper claims registry
entries.summary: Single source of truth for every numerical claim the paper makes (14 claims across 8 categories), each linked to its source artifact + governing gate.

entries.label: Confidence dashboard
entries.summary: Single-screen verdict (42 gates today, all GREEN). The dashboard this catalog sits next to.
